name: Glide No-Code App Builder API
what_it_does: No-code app builder platform with API for creating and managing data-driven apps from spreadsheets, databases, and APIs — enabling teams to build internal tools, client portals, and data apps from Google Sheets, Airtable, or SQL without writing code.
use_cases: Triggering Glide app actions and data mutations via REST API from external systems
Reading and writing data to underlying Glide tables programmatically
Creating webhooks to notify external systems when Glide users take actions
Building agent-accessible internal tools that expose structured data via Glide interfaces
Syncing data between Glide apps and external databases via API
not_for: Complex custom application logic requiring full code control (use React or Vue)
High-performance applications with strict latency requirements
Applications requiring offline-first architecture or native mobile performance
best_when: A team needs to rapidly build internal tools or portals from existing data sources without engineering resources, with API access for external automation.
avoid_when: You need custom UI logic, high performance, or complex business logic beyond what no-code supports.

auth.notes: Bearer token from Glide app settings. App-scoped access. No OAuth — simple token auth. Webhook support for user action events. Table API for reading and mutating data.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: limited apps, 500 rows, Glide branding
pricing.paid_tiers: Maker from $49/month; Team from $99; Business from $249; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Per-app pricing on higher tiers. Row limits on lower plans. Good for internal tools and portals. Strong Google Workspace integration. Growing business-focused app builder.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits per plan; row limits affect data volume

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API covers table data operations (read/write). Webhook support for app events. API is secondary to no-code interface — primarily useful for data sync. Documentation improving with product maturity.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Row creation creates new rows — no built-in deduplication via API. Row updates are idempotent for same values. Data read operations always idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: API is an add-on to the no-code interface — some operations only possible in UI
Row limits on lower plans affect how much data agents can read/write
Data changes via API bypass Glide app logic — business rules must be re-implemented
Glide tables vs. Google Sheets source — understand which is authoritative

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Bearer token auth. SOC2 certified. GDPR compliant. US data residency. Data at rest encrypted. Role-based access within Glide app but API is app-scoped only.
